The article starts by mentioning that Stephen Hawking, after 30 years of arguing that black holes swallow everything, has changed his view. He now says that he and others were wrong, and that black holes don't actually trap information forever. He mentions that this could help solve the "black hole information paradox," which is an important puzzle in modern physics. The paradox is about what happens to information that falls into a black hole. According to Hawking's earlier theory, particles emitted by a black hole (Hawking radiation) don't carry any information about the interior, so when the black hole evaporates, the information is lost.
